sys_properties.description:
Tanium API Token

The API token for the Tanium integration to use for API requests.

The token expiration period should be at least 30 days, and the token should be valid for all IPs (using 0.0.0.0/0 as the valid IP range), or for the CIDR range for your ServiceNow environment (which you can find by contacting ServiceNow support). If this token is not set, then the username and password system properties must be set. Most tasks requiring authentication to the Tanium API will use whichever authentication method is available, so either the token or username/password are both acceptable. If possible, it's recommended to set both the username/password, and API token properties in case something goes wrong with either, to avoid interruption. Doing so also allows Tanium Integration Core to automatically update its own API token.

This token expires every 30 days by default. A scheduled job will update it approximately every 7 days by default. You can adjust this schedule by modifying the linked scheduled job.

To set this API token manually, either for the initial setup or thereafter, please follow the instructions in the Tanium documentation.

Default: (none; must be set)
sys_properties.name: x_taniu_tan_core.tanium_api_token
